user_message: Pulling in context from Coho Winery's escalation email -- need to investigate the decline for the security posture.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-25 10:46 EST
Subject: Re: Re: security posture Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 14:03 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-05 14:16 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Coho Winery security posture

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Coho Winery: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-L trap (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes single-thread input); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-L failure mode (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes single-thread input)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-L -- Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes single-thread input
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
